# botyard_bot (Data Source)

Reads a single Botyard bot by slug within the configured organization.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a single bot by slug within the configured organization.
data "botyard_bot" "example" {
  slug = "my-bot"
}

output "bot_id" {
  value = data.botyard_bot.example.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `slug` (String) Slug of the bot to look up.

### Read-Only

- `config_generation` (Number) Monotonic config generation counter.
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC 3339).
- `desired_state` (String) Control-plane desired lifecycle state.
- `health_status` (String) Health monitoring status.
- `id` (String) Unique bot identifier (UUID).
- `name` (String) Human-readable bot name.
- `namespace` (String) Kubernetes namespace for the bot runtime.
- `onboarding_state` (String) Onboarding state.
- `org_id` (String) Organization ID that owns the bot.
- `runtime_class` (String) Runtime class.
- `runtime_privilege_mode` (String) Runtime privilege mode.
- `storage_class` (String) Storage class.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp (RFC 3339).
